ddls:
CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "server_type" TEXT /* Type of server (e.g., web server, database server) */,
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data center where the server is located */,
  "cpu_cores" INTEGER /* Number of CPU cores */,
  "memory_size_mb" INTEGER /* Total memory size in MB */,
  "storage_capacity_gb" INTEGER /* Total storage capacity in GB */,
  "server_status" TEXT /* Current status of the server (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  "os_version" TEXT /* Operating system version */,
  "last_reboot_time" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the last reboot */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_servers_data_center_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_center_id") REFERENCES data_centers ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_centers" (
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data center */,
  "data_center_name" TEXT /* Name of the data center */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the data center */,
  "manager_name" TEXT /* Name of the data center manager */,
  "manager_email" TEXT /* Email address of the data center manager */,
  "manager_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the data center manager */,
  "max_server_capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum number of servers that can be hosted in the data center */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_resources" (
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system resource */,
  "resource_name" TEXT /* Name of the system resource (e.g., CPU usage, memory usage) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the system resource (e.g., %, MB) */,
  "resource_description" TEXT /* Description of the system resource */,
  "criticality_level" TEXT /* Criticality level of the resource (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_collection" (
  "collection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data collection event */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the server being monitored */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system resource being monitored */,
  "timestamp_ms" INTEGER /* Timestamp of data collection in milliseconds */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the system resource at the time of collection */,
  "collection_method" TEXT /* Method used for data collection (e.g., manual, automated) */,
  "collection_interval_minutes" INTEGER /* Interval in minutes at which the data is collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_collection_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES system_resources ("resource_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_collection_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the system resource being monitored */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value for the system resource */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Alert level (e.g., warning, critical) when the threshold is exceeded */,
  "time_period" TEXT /* Time period the threshold applies to (e.g., last hour, last day) */,
  "notification_method" TEXT /* Method for notifying when the threshold is exceeded (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_thresholds_resource_id FOREIGN KEY ("resource_id") REFERENCES system_resources ("resource_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* ID of the threshold that triggered the alert */,
  "collection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data collection event that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Action taken in response to the alert (e.g., increased resources, rebooted server) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_collection_id FOREIGN KEY ("collection_id") REFERENCES data_collection ("collection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_threshold_id FOREIGN KEY ("threshold_id") REFERENCES thresholds ("threshold_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, viewer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "salt" TEXT /* Salt used for hashing the password */,
  "account_creation_date" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp when the user accessed the platform */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string to identify the device and browser */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
